concept.conceptName: Bicarbonate [Moles/volume] in Urine collected for unspecified duration

en.name: Urinary bicarbonate
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context Urinary bicarbonate is the concentration of bicarbonate in a urine specimen — typically a spot (random) urine sample [1]. The source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp, produced by a biochemistry analyser. Results are reported as a substance concentration in mmol/L (SI unit) or mEq/L; the two are numerically equivalent for bicarbonate [2]. ## Included Concepts The set lists two LOINC "Lab Test" concepts measuring the bicarbonate concentration in a spot urine specimen: - *3008251 / 1964-6 — Bicarbonate [Moles/volume] in Urine (LOINC)*: spot (random) urine sample. Most widely used LOINC code. - *21493400 / 80613-3 — Bicarbonate [Moles/volume] in Urine collected for unspecified duration (LOINC)*: used when the source does not specify whether the sample is spot or timed. **Note on units.** Recommended unit follows LOINC's `EXAMPLE_UCUM_UNITS`: **`mmol/L`** (concept 8753 — millimole per litre). Source systems may report `mmol/L` or `mEq/L`; for bicarbonate (a monovalent anion) the two are numerically equal. ## Excluded Concepts The following LOINC concepts that exist for urinary bicarbonate are deliberately not part of this set, grouped by reason for exclusion. ### Timed urine collections — different specimen (timed vs. spot) - *3048842 / 47580-6 — Bicarbonate [Moles/volume] in 24 hour Urine* ### Excretion rates — different measurand (amount per unit time, not a concentration) - *3052013 / 47579-8 — Bicarbonate [Moles/time] in 24 hour Urine* ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports "urinary bicarbonate", "urine HCO3", "spot urine bicarbonate" without further specification: `3008251 / 1964-6 — Bicarbonate [Moles/volume] in Urine`. By far the most common LOINC code. - **Source reports a 24-hour (or other timed) urine collection**: do **not** map to this concept set. Timed urinary bicarbonate is out of scope. - **Do not confuse with**: bicarbonate in blood (separate concept set, "Bicarbonate in blood"), urinary bicarbonate excretion rate (mmol/24h). ## References [1] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 1964-6 — Bicarbonate [Moles/volume] in Urine. Available: https://loinc.org/1964-6/ [2] Nomenclature for Properties and Units (NPU). IFCC/IUPAC. NPU codes (current release). Available: https://npu-terminology.org/npu-database/
